---
title: "AI Industry Trackers"
description: "Weekly strategy summaries by industry and revenue segment, synthesised from SEC filings, 8-K material events, and news."
---

Strategy context is drawn from 10-Q filings (point-in-time blueprint); material events from 8-K filings (M&A, capex, leadership); current developments from news feeds. Each edition covers three revenue tiers — Fortune 100, PE Mid-Size ($500M–$10B), and Startups — and is synthesised by an LLM into a segment-aware narrative.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import sys, os
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(".")))

try:
    from trackers.io import (
        list_industries_with_summaries,
        list_latest_summaries,
        get_output_dir,
        get_latest_run_metadata,
        get_news_feed_summary,
    )
    industries_with_data = list_industries_with_summaries()
    all_industries = ["consumer", "industrials", "financials"]
except Exception:
    industries_with_data = []
    all_industries = ["consumer", "industrials", "financials"]

# Industry navigation
parts = []
for ind in all_industries:
    if ind in industries_with_data:
        parts.append(f"[{ind.capitalize()}](#{ind})")
    else:
        parts.append(f"{ind.capitalize()} (coming soon)")
print("**View by industry:** " + " · ".join(parts))
print()
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import sys, os
from datetime import datetime
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(".")))

def fmt_date(ymd):
    if not ymd or len(ymd) < 10:
        return ymd
    try:
        d = datetime.strptime(ymd[:10], "%Y-%m-%d")
        return d.strftime("%d %B %Y")
    except Exception:
        return ymd

_SEGMENT_LABELS = {
    "fortune_100": "Fortune 100",
    "pe_mid": "PE Mid-Size ($500M–$10B)",
    "startup": "Startups (VC-backed)",
}

try:
    from trackers.io import (
        list_industries_with_summaries,
        list_latest_summaries,
        get_output_dir,
        get_latest_run_metadata,
        get_news_feed_summary,
        list_8k_signals,
        get_segment_summary,
    )
    industries_with_data = list_industries_with_summaries()
except Exception:
    industries_with_data = []

for industry_id in industries_with_data:
    meta = get_latest_run_metadata(industry_id) or {}
    latest_sec = meta.get("latest_sec_run") or ""
    summaries = list_latest_summaries(industry_id)
    feed_info = get_news_feed_summary(industry_id)

    # Section heading
    print(f'\n## {industry_id.capitalize()} {{#{industry_id}}}\n')

    # Data-as-of and sources (HTML block)
    if latest_sec:
        try:
            d = datetime.strptime(latest_sec[:8], "%Y%m%d")
            t = latest_sec[9:15] if len(latest_sec) >= 15 else ""
            display = d.strftime("%d %B %Y")
            if t:
                display += " at " + t[:2] + ":" + t[2:4] + " UTC"
        except Exception:
            display = latest_sec
    else:
        display = "No data available for this industry yet."
    feed_line = ""
    if feed_info and (feed_info.get("configured_count") or feed_info.get("sources_in_run")):
        cfg = feed_info.get("configured_count") or 0
        srcs = feed_info.get("sources_in_run") or []
        if srcs:
            names = [s.replace("www.", "").replace("feeds.", "").split(".")[0].title() for s in srcs]
            feed_line = f"<p>News sources: {cfg} feed(s) configured; {len(srcs)} contributed to this edition: " + ", ".join(names) + ".</p>"
        else:
            feed_line = f"<p>News sources: {cfg} feed(s) configured; none matched this edition.</p>"
    html_block = f'<div class="tracker-data-as-of"><p><strong>Data as of</strong></p><p>{display}</p>{feed_line}</div>'
    try:
        from IPython.display import HTML, display as ipydisplay
        ipydisplay(HTML(html_block))
    except Exception:
        print(html_block)

    # Segment coverage table
    try:
        seg_summary = get_segment_summary(industry_id)
        if seg_summary:
            print("| Segment | Companies with news coverage |")
            print("|---|---|")
            for seg in ["fortune_100", "pe_mid", "startup"]:
                count = seg_summary.get(seg, 0)
                label = _SEGMENT_LABELS.get(seg, seg)
                print(f"| {label} | {count} |")
            print()
    except Exception:
        pass

    # M&A and material events (from 8-K pipeline)
    try:
        k8_signals = list_8k_signals(industry_id, limit=8)
        ma_signals = [s for s in k8_signals if s.get("is_ma")]
        capex_signals = [s for s in k8_signals if s.get("is_capex") and not s.get("is_ma")]
        if ma_signals or capex_signals:
            print("**Material events this week (from 8-K filings):**\n")
            for s in (ma_signals + capex_signals)[:6]:
                company = s.get("company_name", "")
                seg = s.get("segment", "")
                seg_tag = f"[{_SEGMENT_LABELS.get(seg, seg.upper())}]" if seg else ""
                signal_type = s.get("signal_type", "")
                type_tag = "[M&A]" if signal_type == "ma" else "[Capex]"
                excerpt = str(s.get("excerpt", ""))[:200]
                url = s.get("filing_url", "")
                dollar = s.get("dollar_amount", "")
                dollar_str = f" ({dollar})" if dollar and str(dollar) != "nan" else ""
                if url:
                    print(f"- {type_tag} **[{company}]({url})** {seg_tag}{dollar_str}: {excerpt}")
                else:
                    print(f"- {type_tag} **{company}** {seg_tag}{dollar_str}: {excerpt}")
            print()
    except Exception:
        pass

    # Week / edition links
    if summaries:
        week_links = []
        for i, s in enumerate(summaries):
            date_part = s.get("date", "")
            label = fmt_date(date_part) + (" (latest)" if i == 0 else "")
            week_links.append(f"[{label}](#{industry_id}-{date_part})")
        print("**Editions:** " + " | ".join(week_links))
        print()

    # Per-week summary content
    for i, s in enumerate(summaries):
        date_part = s.get("date", "")
        path = s.get("path", "")
        base = get_output_dir(industry_id, "weekly_summaries")
        full_path = os.path.join(base, path)
        print(f'\n### Week of {fmt_date(date_part)} {{#{industry_id}-{date_part}}}\n')
        if os.path.isfile(full_path):
            with open(full_path) as f:
                body = f.read()
            lines = body.strip().split("\n")
            # Skip file header (title, Week of, strategy line) until ---; show everything after
            start = 0
            for j, line in enumerate(lines):
                if line.strip() == "---":
                    start = j + 1
                    break
                if line.strip().startswith("## "):
                    start = j
                    break
            summary_lines = lines[start:] if start < len(lines) else body.split("\n")
            bumped = []
            for line in summary_lines:
                stripped = line.strip()
                if stripped.startswith("### "):
                    bumped.append("##### " + stripped[4:])
                elif stripped.startswith("## "):
                    bumped.append("#### " + stripped[3:])
                else:
                    bumped.append(line)
            print("\n".join(bumped))
        else:
            print("(Summary file not found.)")
        print()

# Placeholder sections for industries without data yet
for industry_id in ["industrials", "financials"]:
    if industry_id in industries_with_data:
        continue
    print(f'\n## {industry_id.capitalize()} {{#{industry_id}}}\n')
    print("This tracker is in development. Universe and news feeds are configured — run the pipeline to generate the first edition.\n")
```
